name: Catheter infection
description: Catheter infection — infection related to an intravascular catheter (line infection, catheter-related bloodstream infection).

en.name: Catheter infection
en.shortDescription: Catheter infection — infection related to an intravascular catheter (line infection, catheter-related bloodstream infection).
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Catheter infection covers infections related to an intravascular catheter or vascular access device — from local exit-site or tunnel infection to catheter-related bloodstream infection (CRBSI) / central line-associated bloodstream infection (CLABSI). These are common, preventable hospitalisation complications in intensive care, where central venous and haemodialysis catheters are frequent.

## Scope

The set is anchored on the SNOMED concept *43021283 / 473083009 — Infection associated with vascular device* with all descendants included. It therefore captures infections of intravenous and central venous catheters (including exit-site and tunnel-site infections), totally implantable venous access devices, and haemodialysis catheters (tunnelled and temporary), as well as catheter-related/central-line-associated bloodstream infection and intravascular-line candidaemia.

## Mapping Notes

- This set captures infections **of/related to** a vascular catheter or device, not the presence of the catheter itself or its mechanical complications.
- Urinary catheter infection (CAUTI) and peritoneal dialysis catheter infection are intentionally **not** part of this set — the anchor is restricted to vascular devices.

fr.name: Infection de cathéter
fr.shortDescription: Infection de cathéter — infection liée à un cathéter intravasculaire (infection de ligne, bactériémie liée au cathéter).

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Anchor on SNOMED 'Infection associated with vascular device' (473083009) with descendants (replacing the flat list of 6 concepts; now 17 vascular-catheter infection concepts, excluding urinary/peritoneal catheter infections). Subcategory Hospitalization complications; added clinical description.
